title: Healthcare Payment Reform Act
summary: Establishes bundled payment requirements for state Medicaid programs and requires hospitals to accept single payments covering all services in a treatment episode.
excerpt: All healthcare providers shall implement episode-based payment models for listed procedures by January 1, 2026.
full_text: A bill to establish bundled payment requirements for state Medicaid programs, require episode-based reimbursement for qualifying hospital procedures, and direct the state health agency to publish compliance rules.

title: Medicare Advantage Bundling Standards
summary: Mandates pricing transparency for bundled payment arrangements in Medicare Advantage plans operating in Alabama.
excerpt: Bundled payment arrangements must disclose component pricing within 30 days of patient request.
full_text: A bill requiring transparency standards for bundled payment arrangements in Medicare Advantage plans, defining disclosure obligations and civil penalties for noncompliance.

title: Consumer Data Broker Accountability Act
summary: Creates registration, deletion, and opt-out requirements for consumer data brokers and expands attorney general enforcement authority.
excerpt: A data broker shall maintain a public mechanism allowing a consumer to request deletion of covered personal information.
full_text: An act concerning accountability for data brokers, requiring registration, consumer deletion rights, opt-out processes, data minimization standards, and attorney general enforcement authority.

title: Children's Data Minimization and Safety Act
summary: Restricts data collection and targeted advertising practices for online services likely to be accessed by minors.
excerpt: A covered business shall not process a minor's personal information beyond what is reasonably necessary to provide the requested service.
full_text: An act to limit the collection, retention, and targeted advertising use of personal information of minors, establish default privacy settings, and authorize agency rulemaking.

title: Algorithmic Accountability for Data Brokers
summary: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and annual audits for registered data brokers using sensitive personal information.
excerpt: Each data broker shall complete an annual algorithmic impact assessment covering disparate impact, deletion controls, and downstream access governance.
full_text: A bill to require impact assessments and annual audits for data brokers using sensitive personal information and to establish notice, deletion, and appeal procedures for affected consumers.

title: Consumer Deletion Rights for Data Brokers
summary: Would have granted broad deletion rights against data brokers but was narrowed after committee concerns about enforcement complexity.
excerpt: A consumer may compel a data broker to delete all covered records absent a specific statutory retention requirement.
full_text: A bill proposing broad consumer deletion rights for data broker records, broad rulemaking authority, and a private right of action. The measure failed after concerns about compliance cost and litigation exposure.

title: Education Accountability and Appointment Reform
summary: Requires that the teacher standards board submit multiple nominees for executive director appointments and strengthens reporting requirements.
excerpt: The board shall submit no fewer than three nominees to the Board of Education for appointment of the executive director.
full_text: A bill to reform the appointment process for the teacher standards board executive director and require annual performance and transparency reporting to the Board of Education.

title: Hospital Episode Pricing Disclosure Act
summary: Requires hospitals to publish expected episode pricing for common procedures and disclose payer bundle participation.
excerpt: A hospital shall publicly disclose expected episode pricing for each procedure designated by the secretary.
full_text: An act requiring public disclosure of episode pricing for designated hospital procedures and reporting on payer participation in bundled reimbursement arrangements.

title: Medicaid Episode Cost Containment Act
summary: Creates episode-based reimbursement pilots for Medicaid services and directs the agency to phase in bundled payment rules.
excerpt: The department shall establish pilot episode-based reimbursement models for no fewer than three service categories.
full_text: A bill to launch pilot episode-based reimbursement programs for Medicaid services, require agency reporting, and phase in compliance rules for provider participants.

title: Grid Reliability and Emissions Reporting Act
summary: Requires large utilities to publish reliability metrics and facility-level emissions reporting during emergency events.
excerpt: Each covered utility shall file an annual reliability and event-based emissions report with the commission.
full_text: An act requiring annual reliability reports and event-based emissions reporting for large utilities, including outage metrics and facility-level greenhouse gas disclosures.

title: Tenant Utility Cost Relief and Transparency Act
summary: Would require landlords to standardize utility pass-through billing and provide line-item transparency to tenants.
excerpt: A landlord that passes through utility costs shall provide a monthly line-item statement describing the basis for each charge.
full_text: A bill establishing disclosure standards for landlord utility pass-through charges, tenant billing transparency, and administrative enforcement for noncompliance.

title: Data Broker Private Enforcement Act
summary: Would have created a private right of action against data brokers for unauthorized sale of consumer data.
excerpt: Any person injured by a violation of this chapter may bring a civil action for damages and injunctive relief.
full_text: A failed bill that would have established private enforcement rights against data brokers for unauthorized collection, sale, or retention of consumer information.
